response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong systems-level instincts (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, event-driven architectures) and solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD), but his professional C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial-automation domain knowledge (IEC 62443, MQTT, SDL practices), none of which appear on the resume. His recent decade has centered on TypeScript/Node web platforms, making him a weak fit for a senior C++ industrial role despite his overall seniority.
strengths: Deep experience with high-performance, low-latency, event-driven systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, testable design, plus mentoring and workshop leadership
Broad full-stack and embedded-adjacent versatility with 15+ years of professional experience
concerns: Minimal recent professional C++ — last dedicated C/C++ role ended in 2015; C++ absent from skills list
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, or IEC 62443 / SDL security-standard experience evident
Recent focus is web/TypeScript product work, misaligned with networked multi-threaded C++ platform development
